dashboard.label: Threat Actor Chain Narrative
dashboard.description: Rogue-actor storyline view: expected chain stages vs observed events, agent handoffs, and per-stage risk statements.

search.query: `acme_genai_index` $time_range$ testbed_mode=KILL_CHAIN_ACTIVE incident_id=* | search chain_id="$chain_id$" OR "$chain_id$"="*" | stats earliest(_time) as start latest(_time) as end values(threat_actor_profile) as threat_actor values(chain_name) as chain_name values(scenario_family) as family values(technique_id) as technique_chain values(agent.id) as agents max(framework.cvss_score) as max_cvss dc(stage_num) as stages_seen by incident_id | eval technique_chain_str=mvjoin(technique_chain, " → ") | sort - start

`acme_genai_index` $time_range$ incident_id="$selected_incident$" testbed_mode=KILL_CHAIN_ACTIVE | sort stage_num, _time | lookup acme_kill_chain_stages_lookup chain_id, stage_num OUTPUT stage_name as expected_stage | table _time, stage_num, stage_name, expected_stage, technique_id, framework.technique_name, requesting_agent_id, target_agent_id, agent.id, rogue_actor_story, risk_statement, defenseclaw_action, execution_mode

html.p: Each incident_id is one rogue-actor campaign. Stages should align with acme_kill_chain_stages_lookup. Gaps between expected and observed stages indicate detection or emulation gaps worth teaching in workshops.
